architecture.summary: Transcoders are wide, sparsely-activating MLP layers trained to faithfully approximate a model's original dense MLP sublayer output. They use an encoder-decoder architecture (Eqs. 3-4) where sparse feature activations are computed via dot-product of input with encoder vectors, then scaled decoder vectors are summed to reconstruct MLP output. The key innovation is enabling weights-based circuit analysis through MLPs by factorizing feature-to-feature attributions into input-invariant (decoder·encoder dot product) and input-dependent (feature activation) terms, allowing general input-output behavior analysis impossible with SAEs.

biblio.title: Transcoders Find Interpretable LLM Feature Circuits

claims.statement: Transcoders enable input-invariant feature-level circuit analysis through MLP sublayers, cleanly factorizing circuits into input-invariant and input-dependent terms, which SAEs cannot achieve.
On blind interpretability evaluation of 50 random features each from Pythia-410M layer 15 transcoder and MLP-in SAE, transcoders showed 41 interpretable features versus 38 for SAE.
Transcoders achieve equal or better sparsity-accuracy Pareto frontier than SAEs across GPT2-small, Pythia-410M, and Pythia-1.4B, with the gap widening on larger models.

Transcoders successfully reverse-engineer unknown circuits, deriving that layer 8 feature 355 in GPT2-small fires on semicolons in parenthetical citations via input-invariant de-embedding and circuit-finding algorithm without viewing token text.

claims.dataset: Greater-than circuit (100 prompts: 'The war lasted from 17YY to 17')
claims.statement: On the GPT2-small greater-than circuit task, using only the top 23 transcoder features (versus many more neurons) recovers most of the original model's performance on the probability difference metric.

The circuit analysis method does not engage with how attention patterns are computed and treats them as fixed; extending transcoders to understand attention softmax computation is noted as future work.
Transcoders, like SAEs, are approximations to the underlying model and the resulting error may lose key information; the paper leaves detailed comparison of reconstruction errors to future work.

Nine blind case studies demonstrate transcoders can infer model behavior on unknown tasks using input-invariant information (de-embeddings, input-invariant feature connections) without viewing activation examples.
